//: Mutation testing for the exlib simulation core. Run from this folder, PINNING the framework: `dotnet stryker --target-framework net10.0` (required post-legacy: csprojs read TargetFramework from the $(CurrentGameTfm)/$(AllGameTfms) MSBuild vars, which Stryker's analyzer can't expand → 'No project found').
//gate: Use --break-at <pct> in CI to fail below a mutation-score floor once it is raised.
//scope-it: Run ONE small file at a time, e.g. `--mutate **/Blocks/Machines/GraceTimer.cs` (58% as of 2026-06-21). At a LARGE mutate scope (hundreds of mutants) Stryker's per-test coverage attribution degrades in this repo's custom-OutDir/TFM build (per-mutant time collapses to ~0.1s and mutants default to Survived) - the whole-config score under-reports badly. Trust scoped runs only.
//limitation: Only exlib (the leaf project) mutates cleanly. ppex/smex use the ExpandedLib.Generators Roslyn generator, which Stryker's in-memory recompile can't run ('Internal error due to compile error', CS0535 on generated partial members). Materializing the generated *.g.cs + disabling the generator gets ppex to BUILD, but its single big files (e.g. PipeNetwork.cs, 203 mutants) then hit the same at-scale attribution collapse. Keep ppex/smex guarantees on the integration/property tests + a manual mutant check (revert the guard, confirm the regression test goes red).
